title: Visualizing Totally Bounded Sets in a Metric Space
description: A diagram showing how a set can be covered by a finite number of ε-balls, illustrating the concept of total boundedness.
elements: A set S in a metric space (X, d)
Open balls B(x_i, ε) of radius ε centered at points x_i
A finite set of center points {x_1, x_2, ..., x_n}
relationships: The set S is completely contained within the union of the finite collection of ε-balls
Each point in S is within distance ε of at least one center point x_i
This covering property must hold for every possible ε > 0 for S to be totally bounded
